sections.paragraph:
fut. יָחֹן and like regular verbs יֶחֱנַן Amo 5:15 the former with suffix יְחָנֵּנוּ Psa 67:2 123:2 יְחֻנֶּנּוּ Isa 27:11 but with suffix 2 pers. יָחְנְךָ for יְחָנְךָ Gen 43:29 Isa 30:19 inf. absol. חָנֹון Isa. loc. cit., constr. with suff. חֲנַנְכֶם Isa 30:18, with חֶנְנֶהּ Psa 102:14.

(1) to be inclined towards (compare the kindred חָנָה), hence to be favourably inclined, to favour some one, to be gracious to, to pity. (Arab. حَنَّ to feel desire, or commiseration towards any one; followed by الى, على Followed by an acc., Exo 33:19 Lam 4:16 Pro 14:31. חָנֵּנִי, חָנֵּנוּ (once חָנְנֵנִי Psa 9:14), have mercy on me, on us; Psa 4:2 6:3 31:10 .

(2) to give some one anything graciously, followed by two acc. of pers. and thing, Gen 33:5 Psa 119:29 Jud. 21:22 acc. of pers., Pro 19:17 absol. Psa 37:21, 26 Psa 37:26. As to Job 19:17, see חַנָּה No. 2 .

Niphal נֵחַן (of the form נֵאַר from the root אָרַר, נֵחַם from the root חָמַם; see Lehrgeb. p. 371), to be compassionated, to be an object of pity, Jer 22:23 pass. of Poel No. 2.

Piel, to make acceptable (compare חֵן, חִין), Pro 26:25.

Poel-

(1) i.q. Kal No. 2, Pro 14:21.

(2) to compassionate, to lament for, Psa 102:15.

Hophal, to receive favour, to be favoured, i.q. מָצָא חֵן Witleid, Gnade finden, Pro 21:10 Isa 26:10.

Hithpael, to intreat for mercy, followed by לְ of pers., Est 4:8 Job 19:16, אֶל 1Ki 8:33, 47 1Ki 8:47 Job 8:5 Psa 30:9, and לִפְנֵי 1Ki 8:59 9:3 2Ch 6:24.

Derivatives (besides those immediately following), חֵן, חִין, חַנָּה, חַנּוּן, חֲנִינָה, חִנָּם, תְּחִנָּה, תַּחֲנוּנִים, and the pr.n. חָנוּן, חַנִּיאֵל, יְחֹוחָנָן [and חֵנָדָד, חַנָּתֹן].
